steps.text: Some letters are extra special — they're called vowels. Every word needs at least one! Let's trace them.

steps.feedback: a! You'll find this in almost every word.
e! The most common letter in English.
i! Don't forget the dot on top.
o! A perfect circle.
u! Like a little cup.

text: a, e, i, o, u — you traced all five vowels! Brilliant!

steps.text: These letters all start the same way — with a round curve going anti-clockwise. Watch carefully!
Find c and trace it — start at the top and go round!
steps.feedback: c! That curving stroke is the key.

steps.text: Now find o — all the way round this time!
steps.feedback: o! A full circle, anti-clockwise.

steps.text: Find a — it starts just like c!
steps.feedback: a! Round first, then the straight bit down.

steps.text: Now find d — round first, then tall!
steps.feedback: d! Same round start, but the line goes up high.
g! Round like the others, then the tail dips below.

text: c, o, a, d, g — you mastered the round letters! Brilliant!

steps.text: The alphabet has a special order. Let's trace the very first five letters!
Find a — the first letter of the alphabet!
steps.feedback: a! Right at the start.
b! A tall letter — the line goes up high.
c! The round one.
d! Tall again, but faces the other way to b.
e! a, b, c, d, e — five in a row!

text: a, b, c, d, e — the first five letters! Brilliant!

steps.text: Numbers have shapes too — let's trace them in order!

steps.feedback: 1! One straight line.
2! A curve and a slide.
3! Two bumps facing right.
4! Down, across, then tall.
5! Straight down, belly out, hat on top.

steps.text: You know 1 to 5 — now let's trace 6 all the way to 10! We use 0 for the zero in 10.

steps.feedback: 6! Curves round and closes at the bottom.
7! Across the top, then a diagonal line down.
8! Two loops on top of each other.
9! A circle on top, then a tail going down.
0! A full oval — the zero that makes 10!

text: 6, 7, 8, 9, 0 — you can write all ten digits! Brilliant!
